free_text: Prompt fission product yields in the 238U(n,f) reaction
Autorite de Surete Nucleaire, Montrouge
Experiment performed at the Los
Alamos Neutron Science Center's Weapons Neutron
Research facility's spallation neutron source
The 238U target consisted of two
foils, 840 mg/cm2 thick in total. Four natural Fe
foils, 165 mg/cm2 thick in total, were placed two in
front and two in back of the 238U foils so that the
cross section of the strong 846.8 keV line of 56Fe
from inelastic scattering was used as a check on the
cross sections obtained
Gamma rays produced in the bombardment of the
238U target by neutrons were measured with the
GErmanium Array for Neutron-Induced Excitations
GEANIE spectrometer. GEANIE was comprised of eleven
Compton-suppressed planar Ge detectors (low energy
photon spectrometers, or LEPS) , nine Compton
suppressed coaxial Ge detectors, and six unsuppressed
coaxial Ge detectors.
The energy of the neutrons was determined by
using the time-of-flight technique. The beam time
structure consisted of 725 mu-s long macropulses at
40 Hz rate. Each macropulse contained approximately
416 micropulses spaced every 1.8 mu-s. HPGe detector
array GEANIE was located on the 60R (60deg- Right)
flight path at the distance of 20.34 m from the
spallation target.
The neutrons were produced in a natural W
spallation target driven by an 800 MeV proton beam.
Statistical uncertainty
Compiled by S.H.
